name: First March
brief: Slim pieces run faster: your lance is a line (speed 3) while the hollowed slab only manages 2. Close the distance and run it down.

name: Broadside
brief: A strike deals damage per cell of contact. Point-first your lance would poke for 1; laid flat against the wide slab it lands 3 cells at once and kills in a single blow.

name: Full Extension
brief: Attack reach equals speed: slide up to speed minus one cells, then strike one further. The wounded keep sits exactly three cells out — slide two, strike the third.

name: Fold to Spear
brief: Folding reshapes your box around a shared cell. Turn the flat lance into a four-deep spear and your tip leaps three ranks up the board in a single action.

name: One Blow, Two Graves
brief: A strike footprint damages every enemy piece it touches. Line up both hollowed slabs under your lance's shadow and cleave the pair with one strike.

name: High Ground
brief: Damage per cell of contact is the lesser of the two heights. Your two-high keep crushes the wounded keep for 2 per cell where a flat piece would only scratch for 1.

name: Re-forge
brief: A wounded piece still lumbers around in its old fat shape. Fold your hollowed slab compact into a true lance — measure 4, volume 4 — and it speeds up from 2 to 3.

name: Tempo
brief: A fold costs your entire turn. With the clock at two plies there is no time to re-forge — strike now or the game is adjudicated out from under you.

name: The Picket Line
brief: No piece may pass through another, friend or foe. The picket ahead of your lance must fall before you can reach the slab hiding behind it.

name: Waste Not
brief: Damage beyond a piece's remaining measure simply evaporates. Let the keep's heavy fist flatten the scrap beside it, and send the lance after the real prize.

name: Race the Clock
brief: When the ply cap lands, the board is adjudicated on total measure — but this campaign demands annihilation. Hunt down both slabs before time is called.

name: The Last Field
brief: Everything at once: the north fields its full array, hollowed and brittle. Fold, cleave and crush — leave nothing standing.
